editableNewDialogue.text: Can you help me write a short message to congratulate someone on a new job?

Sure—what’s the tone (formal, friendly, funny), and is it for a text, card, or email? If you want a quick default: “Congratulations on the new job! You’ve earned this, and I know you’re going to do amazing. Wishing you a great start and lots of success in your new role.”
Make it more formal, like for an email.

Subject: Congratulations on Your New Position

Dear [Name],

Congratulations on your new position at [Company]. This is a well-deserved opportunity, and I’m confident you will make a strong impact in your new role. Wishing you continued success as you begin this next chapter.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
Also, quick question: what date is World Teachers’ Day?
World Teachers’ Day is October 5 each year (established by UNESCO). Note that some countries also celebrate separate national Teachers’ Days on different dates.
Give me one sentence I can say to a teacher on that day.
“Thank you for your dedication and patience—your guidance makes a lasting difference in your students’ lives.”
Random: I heard the Eiffel Tower gets taller in summer—is that true?
Yes—because metal expands with heat, the Eiffel Tower can become slightly taller in warm weather (often by several centimeters).
I’m thinking of sending her a long voice note tonight instead of another call—our schedules keep clashing, especially with the time difference.

A long voice note can be a great middle ground when timing is hard. You can make it feel personal by sharing a few highlights from your day, asking her a couple of specific questions, and ending with something affectionate—then you both can reply when free.
About those texts—when I said “my girlfriend,” I didn’t mean I actually have one. I was just asking for sample messages.
Got it—so you don’t currently have a girlfriend, you just wanted ready-to-send examples. Tell me the vibe you want (sweet, short, funny, or romantic) and I’ll write a few templates.
Yes, exactly. No girlfriend right now—just templates.
